name: natural

metadata.name: natural
metadata.summary: Convert data to their natural (human-readable) format

documentation:
# Example Usage

Basic usage:

```
>>> from natural.file import accessed
>>> print accessed(__file__)
just now
```

We speak your language (with [your support]):

```
>>> import locale
>>> locale.setlocale(locale.LC_MESSAGES, 'nl_NL')
>>> print accessed(__file__)
zojuist
```

# Bugs/Features

You can issue a ticket in GitHub: <https://github.com/tehmaze/natural/issues>

# Documentation

The project documentation can be found at <http://natural.rtfd.org/>

[your support]: http://natural.readthedocs.org/en/latest/locales.html

modules.name: natural.language
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: natural.language._multi
documentation: Provides translations for plural and singular forms of a term.

functions.name: natural.size.filesize
documentation:
Convert a file size into natural readable format. Multiple formats are
supported.

```
param value

size

param format

default decimal, choices binary, decimal or
gnu

param digits

default 2
```

```pycon
>>> filesize(123)
'123.00 B'
>>> filesize(123456)
'120.56 kB'
>>> filesize(1234567890)
'1.15 GB'
```

functions.name: natural.size.decimalsize
documentation:
Wrapper for {py:func}`filesize`.

```pycon
>>> decimalsize(123)
'123.00 B'
>>> decimalsize(123456)
'120.56 kB'
>>> decimalsize(1234567890)
'1.15 GB'
```

functions.name: natural.size.binarysize
documentation:
Wrapper for {py:func}`filesize`.

```pycon
>>> binarysize(123)
'123.00 iB'
>>> binarysize(123456)
'123.46 KiB'
>>> binarysize(1234567890)
'1.23 GiB'
```

functions.name: natural.size.gnusize
documentation:
Wrapper for {py:func}`filesize`.

```pycon
>>> gnusize(123)
'123.0B'
>>> gnusize(123456)
'120.6K'
>>> gnusize(1234567890)
'1.1G'
```

modules.name: natural.text
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: natural.text.code
documentation:
Transform a sentence using the code spelling alphabet, multiple
international code alphabets are supported.

| format | description                                                                                                                                                                |
| ------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| army   | US (international) army code alphabet                                                                                                                                      |
| faa    | Federal Aviation Administration code alphabet, as described in "ICAO Phonetics in the FAA ATC Manual, §2-4-16"                                                             |
| icao   | International Civil Aviation Organization, as described in "Annex 10 to the Convention on International Civil Aviation, Volume II (Fifth edition, 1995), Chapter 5, 38–40" |
| itu    | International Telecommunication Union Roman alphabet, as described in "ITU Phonetic Alphabet and Figure Code"                                                              |
| morse  | International morse code, as described in "International Morse code Recommendation ITU-R M.1677-1", <http://itu.int/>                                                      |
| word   | International Civil Aviation Organization, as described in "Annex 10 to the Convention on International Civil Aviation, Volume II (Fifth edition, 1995), Chapter 5, 38–40" |

```
param sentence

input sentence

param pad

default None (reside to per-alphabet defaults)

param format

default army
```

```pycon
>>> code('Python')
'PAH pah  YANG kee  TANG go  HO tell  OSS car  NOH vem ber'
>>> code('Python', format='faa')
'PAHPAH  YANGKEY  TANGGO  HOHTELL  OSSCAH  NOVEMBER'
>>> code('Python', format='icao')
'PAH PAH  YANG KEY  TANG GO  HOH TELL  OSS CAH  NO VEM BER'
>>> code('Python', format='itu')
'PAH PAH  YANG KEY  TANG GO  HOH TELL  OSS CAH  NO VEM BER'
>>> code('Python', format='morse')
'.--.  -.--  -  ....  ---  -.'
>>> code('Python', format='word')
'papa  yankee  tango  hotel  oscar  november'
```

functions.name: natural.text.morse
documentation:
Wrapper for {func}`code`.

```pycon
>>> morse('Python')
'.--. -.-- - .... --- -.'
```

functions.name: natural.text.nato
documentation:
Transform a sentence using the NATO spelling alphabet.

```
param sentence

input sentence

param pad

default u' '

param format

default telephony, options telephony or phonetic
```

```pycon
>>> nato('Python')
'papa yankee tango hotel oscar november'
>>> nato('Python', format='phonetic')
'pah-pah yang-key tang-go hoh-tel oss-cah no-vem-ber'
```

functions.name: natural.text.spell
documentation:
Transform a sentence using the localised spelling alphabet.

```
param sentence

input sentence

param pad

default u'  '
```

```pycon
>>> spell('Python')
'Paris  Yokohama  Tripoli  Havanna  Oslo  New York'
```

functions.name: natural.text.pronounce
documentation:
Transform a sentence using the pronounciations of the international code
spelling alphabet. This function is subject to change its behaviour to
support internationalised pronounciations of letters.

```
param sentence

input sentence

param pad

default u'  '
```

```pycon
>>> pronounce('abc')
'ælfɑ ˈbrɑːˈvo ˈtʃɑːli'
```

classes.name: natural.text.Alphabet

methods.name: natural.text.Alphabet.from_pair
documentation:
Returns a new {func}`Alphabet` object for the translation items
specified in `keys` and `values`.
Helper class for (spelling) alphabets.

functions.name: natural.file.accessed
documentation:
Retrieve how long ago a file has been accessed.

```
param filename

name of the file
```

```pycon
>>> print accessed(__file__)        # doctest: +SKIP
just now
```

functions.name: natural.file.created
documentation:
Retrieve how long ago a file has been created.

```
param filename

name of the file
```

```pycon
>>> print created('/')             # doctest: +SKIP
8 weeks ago
```

functions.name: natural.file.modified
documentation:
Retrieve how long ago a file has been modified.

```
param filename

name of the file
```

```pycon
>>> print modified('/')             # doctest: +SKIP
3 days ago
```

functions.name: natural.file.size
documentation:
Retrieve the size of a file.

```
param filename

name of the file
```

```pycon
>>> size('/etc/mime.types')         # doctest: +SKIP
23.70 kB
```

functions.name: natural.util.luhn_checksum
documentation:
Calculates the Luhn checksum for `number`

```
param number

string or int

param chars

string
```

```pycon
>>> luhn_checksum(1234)
4
```

functions.name: natural.util.luhn_calc
documentation:
Calculate the Luhn check digit for `number`.

```
param number

string

param chars

string
```

```pycon
>>> luhn_calc('42')
'2'
```

functions.name: natural.util.luhn_append
documentation:
Append the Luhn check digit of `number` to the input.

```
param number

string

param chars

string
```

```pycon
>>> luhn_append('666')
'6668'
```

functions.name: natural.util.strip
documentation:
Removes characters in `chars` from input `value`.

```
param value

string

param chars

iterable
```

```pycon
>>> strip('hello.world', '.')
'helloworld'
```

functions.name: natural.util.to_decimal
documentation:
Converts a number to a string of decimals in base 10.

```pycon
>>> to_decimal(123)
'123'
>>> to_decimal('o123')
'83'
>>> to_decimal('b101010')
'42'
>>> to_decimal('0x2a')
'42'
```

modules.name: natural.constant
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: natural.number.ordinal
documentation:
Converts a number to its ordinal representation.

```
param value

number
```

```pycon
>>> ordinal(1)
'1st'
>>> ordinal(11)
'11th'
>>> ordinal(101)
'101st'
>>> ordinal(104)
'104th'
>>> ordinal(113)
'113th'
>>> ordinal(123)
'123rd'
```

functions.name: natural.number.double
documentation:
Converts a number to a formatted double based on the current locale.

```
param value

number

param digits

default 2
```

```pycon
>>> double(42)
'42.00'
>>> double(42, digits=1)
'42.0'
>>> double(12.34)
'12.34'
>>> double(1234.56)
'1,234.56'
```

functions.name: natural.number.number
documentation:
Converts a number to a formatted number based on the current locale.

```
param value

number
```

```pycon
>>> number(42)
'42'
>>> number(12.34)
'12'
>>> number(1234)
'1,234'
>>> number(1234567)
'1,234,567'
```

functions.name: natural.number.percentage
documentation:
Converts a fraction to a formatted percentage.

```
param value

number

param digits

default 2
```

```pycon
>>> percentage(1)
'100.00 %'
>>> percentage(0.23, digits=0)
'23 %'
>>> percentage(23.421)
'2,342.10 %'
```

functions.name: natural.number.word
documentation:
Converts a large number to a formatted number containing the textual suffix
for that number.

```
param value

number
```

```pycon
>>> word(1)
'1'
>>> word(123456789)
'123.46 million'
```

modules.name: natural.data
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: natural.data._termsize
documentation: Get the current terminal size, returns a `(height, width)` tuple.

functions.name: natural.data.hexdump
documentation:
Display stream contents in hexadecimal and ASCII format. The `stream`
specified must either be a file-like object that supports the `read`
method to receive bytes, or it can be a string.

To dump a file:

```
>>> hexdump(file(filename))     # doctest: +SKIP
```

Or to dump stdin:

```
>>> import sys
>>> hexdump(sys.stdin)          # doctest: +SKIP
```

```
param stream

stream input
```

functions.name: natural.data.printable
documentation:
Return a printable string from the input `sequence`

```
param sequence

byte or string sequence
```

```pycon
>>> printable('\x1b[1;34mtest\x1b[0m')
'.[1;34mtest.[0m'
>>> printable('\x00\x01\x02\x03\x04\x05\x06\x06')
'........'
>>> printable('12345678')
'12345678'
>>> printable('testing\n')
'testing.'
```

functions.name: natural.data.sparkline
documentation:
Return a spark line for the given data set.

```
value data

sequence of numeric values
```

```pycon
>>> print sparkline([1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 5, 4, 3, 1, 5, 6])  # doctest: +SKIP
▁▂▃▄▅▆▅▄▃▁▅▆
```

functions.name: natural.data.throughput
documentation:
Return the throughput in (intelli)bytes per second.

```
param sample

number of samples sent

param window

default 1, sample window in seconds or
 object

param format

default 'decimal', see 
```

```pycon
>>> throughput(123456, 42)
'2.87 kB/s'
```

modules.name: setup
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: natural.bank.bban_compact
documentation:
Printable compacted Basic Bank Account Number. Removes all the padding
characters.

```
param number

string
```

```pycon
>>> bban_compact('1234.56.78.90')
'1234567890'
>>> bban_compact('068-9999995-01')
'068999999501'
```

functions.name: natural.bank.bban_base10
documentation:
Printable Basic Bank Account Number in base-10.

```
param number

string
```

```pycon
>>> bban_base10('01234567')
'45670123'
>>> bban_base10('ABCD')
'10111213'
```

functions.name: natural.bank.bban
documentation:
Printable Basic Bank Account Number (BBAN) for the given country code. The
`country` must be a valid ISO 3166-2 country code.

```
param value

string or int

param country

string
```

```pycon
>>> bban('068-9999995-01', 'BE')
'068999999501'
>>> bban('555', 'NL')
'555'
>>> bban('555', 'NL', validate=True)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: Invalid BBAN, number does not match specification
>>> bban('123', 'XY', validate=True)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: Invalid BBAN, country unknown
```

functions.name: natural.bank.iban
documentation:
Printable International Bank Account Number (IBAN) as specified in ISO
13616\.

```
param number

string
```

```pycon
>>> iban('BE43068999999501')
'BE43 0689 9999 9501'
>>> iban('XY32012341234123', validate=True)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: Invalid IBAN, country unknown
>>> iban('BE43068999999502', validate=True)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: Invalid IBAN, digits check failed
```

functions.name: natural.phone.e123
documentation:
Printable E.123 (Notation for national and international telephone numbers
from ITU) numbers.

```
param number

string

param areasize

int

param groupsize

int

param national

bool
```

```pycon
>>> e123(155542315678)
'+1 555 4231 5678'
>>> e123('+31654231567', areasize=1)
'+31 6 5423 1567'
>>> e123('+3114020', areasize=2)
'+31 14 020'
>>> e123('+312054231567', areasize=2, national=True)
'(020) 5423 1567'
```

functions.name: natural.phone.e161
documentation:
Printable a 26 Latin letters (A to Z) phone number to the 12-key telephone
keypad number.

```
param number

string

param alphabet

dict
```

```pycon
>>> e161('0800-PIZZA123')
'080074992123'
>>> e161('0800^PIZZA123')
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: Character "^" (0x5e) is not in the E.161 alphabet
```

functions.name: natural.phone.e164
documentation:
Printable E.164 (The international public telecommunication numbering plan
from ITU) numbers.

```
param number

string
```

```pycon
>>> e164(155542315678)
'+155542315678'
>>> e164('+31 20 5423 1567')
'+312054231567'
```

functions.name: natural.phone.enum
documentation:
Printable DNS ENUM (telephone number mapping) record.

```
param number

string

param zone

string
```

```pycon
>>> enum('+31 20 5423 1567')
'7.6.5.1.3.2.4.5.0.2.1.3.e164.arpa.'
>>> enum('+31 97 99 6642', zone='e164.spacephone.org')
'2.4.6.6.9.9.7.9.1.3.e164.spacephone.org.'
```

functions.name: natural.phone.imei
documentation:
Printable International Mobile Station Equipment Identity (IMEI) numbers.

```
param number

string or int
```

```pycon
>>> imei(12345678901234)
'12-345678-901234-7'
>>> imei(1234567890123456)
'12-345678-901234-56'
```

functions.name: natural.phone.imsi
documentation:
Printable International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI) numbers. Mind
that there is no validation done on the actual correctness of the MCC/MNC.
If you wish to validate IMSI numbers, take a look at [python-stdnum].

```
param number

string or int
```

```pycon
>>> imsi(2042312345)
'204-23-12345'
```

[python-stdnum]: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/python-stdnum/

functions.name: natural.phone.meid
documentation:
Printable Mobile Equipment Identifier (MEID) number.

```pycon
>>> meid(123456789012345678)
'1B 69B4BA 630F34 6'
>>> meid('1B69B4BA630F34')
'1B 69B4BA 630F34 6'
```

functions.name: natural.phone.pesn
documentation:
Printable Pseudo Electronic Serial Number.

```
param number

hexadecimal string
```

```pycon
>>> pesn('1B69B4BA630F34E')
'805F9EF7'
```

functions.name: natural.date._total_seconds
documentation:
Takes a `datetime.timedelta` object and returns the delta in seconds.

```pycon
>>> _total_seconds(datetime.timedelta(23, 42, 123456))
1987242
>>> _total_seconds(datetime.timedelta(23, 42, 654321))
1987243
```

functions.name: natural.date._to_datetime
documentation:
Internal function that tries whatever to convert `t` into a
{class}`datetime.datetime` object.

```pycon
>>> _to_datetime('2013-12-11')
datetime.datetime(2013, 12, 11, 0, 0)
>>> _to_datetime('Wed, 11 Dec 2013')
datetime.datetime(2013, 12, 11, 0, 0)
>>> _to_datetime('Wed, 11 Dec 13')
datetime.datetime(2013, 12, 11, 0, 0)
>>> _to_datetime('2012-06-13T15:24:17')
datetime.datetime(2012, 6, 13, 15, 24, 17)
```

functions.name: natural.date._to_date
documentation:
Internal function that tries whatever to convert `t` into a
{class}`datetime.date` object.

```pycon
>>> _to_date('2013-12-11')
datetime.date(2013, 12, 11)
>>> _to_date('Wed, 11 Dec 2013')
datetime.date(2013, 12, 11)
>>> _to_date('Wed, 11 Dec 13')
datetime.date(2013, 12, 11)
```

functions.name: natural.date.delta
documentation:
Calculates the estimated delta between two time objects in human-readable
format. Used internally by the {func}`day` and {func}`duration` functions.

```
param t1

timestamp,  or 
object

param t2

timestamp,  or 
object

param words

default True, allow words like "yesterday", "tomorrow"
and "just now"

param justnow

default datetime.timedelta(seconds=10),
 object representing tolerance for
considering a delta as meaning 'just now'
```

```pycon
>>> delta(_to_datetime('2012-06-13T15:24:17'), _to_datetime('2013-12-11T12:34:56'))
('77 weeks', -594639)
```

functions.name: natural.date.day
documentation:
Date delta compared to `t`. You can override `now` to specify what date
to compare to.

You can override the date format by supplying a `format` parameter.

```
param t

timestamp,  or 
object

param now

default None, optionally a 
object

param format

default '%B %d'
```

```pycon
>>> import time
>>> day(time.time())
'today'
>>> day(time.time() - 86400)
'yesterday'
>>> day(time.time() - 604800)
'last week'
>>> day(time.time() + 86400)
'tomorrow'
>>> day(time.time() + 604800)
'next week'
```

functions.name: natural.date.duration
documentation:
Time delta compared to `t`. You can override `now` to specify what time
to compare to.

```
param t

timestamp,  or 
object

param now

default None, optionally a 
object

param precision

default 1, number of fragments to return

param words

default None, allow words like "yesterday", if set to
None this will be enabled if precision is set to
1

param justnow

default datetime.timedelta(seconds=10),
 object passed to 
representing tolerance for considering argument t as
meaning 'just now'
```

```pycon
>>> import time
>>> from datetime import datetime
>>> duration(time.time() + 1)
'just now'
>>> duration(time.time() + 11)
'11 seconds from now'
>>> duration(time.time() - 1)
'just now'
>>> duration(time.time() - 11)
'11 seconds ago'
>>> duration(time.time() - 3601)
'an hour ago'
>>> duration(time.time() - 7201)
'2 hours ago'
>>> duration(time.time() - 1234567)
'2 weeks ago'
>>> duration(time.time() + 7200, precision=1)
'2 hours from now'
>>> duration(time.time() - 1234567, precision=3)
'2 weeks, 6 hours, 56 minutes ago'
>>> duration(datetime(2014, 9, 8), now=datetime(2014, 9, 9))
'yesterday'
>>> duration(datetime(2014, 9, 7, 23), now=datetime(2014, 9, 9))
'1 day ago'
>>> duration(datetime(2014, 9, 10), now=datetime(2014, 9, 9))
'tomorrow'
>>> duration(datetime(2014, 9, 11, 1), now=datetime(2014, 9, 9, 23))
'1 day from now'
```

functions.name: natural.date.compress
documentation:
Convert the input to compressed format, works with a
{class}`datetime.timedelta` object or a number that represents the number
of seconds you want to compress. If you supply a timestamp or a
{class}`datetime.datetime` object, it will give the delta relative to the
current time.

You can enable showing a sign in front of the compressed format with the
`sign` parameter, the default is not to show signs.

Optionally, you can chose to pad the output. If you wish your values to be
separated by spaces, set `pad` to `' '`.

```
param t

seconds or  object

param sign

default False

param pad

default ''
```

```pycon
>>> compress(1)
'1s'
>>> compress(12)
'12s'
>>> compress(123)
'2m3s'
>>> compress(1234)
'20m34s'
>>> compress(12345)
'3h25m45s'
>>> compress(123456)
'1d10h17m36s'
```
